title: Claypot-cooked Creamy Egyptian Grass Biryani
description: A rich and creamy Egyptian-inspired dish featuring matcha and claypot-cooked basmati rice, perfect for a romantic dinner.

ingredients.name: Basmati rice
ingredients.notes: long-grain basmati rice is essential for this dish

ingredients.name: Matcha powder
ingredients.notes: use high-quality matcha powder for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Vegetable oil
ingredients.notes: use a neutral-tasting oil like canola or grapeseed

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: finely chop the onion for even cooking

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: mince the garlic for maximum flavor

ingredients.name: Chickpeas
ingredients.notes: canned or cooked chickpeas work well in this dish

ingredients.name: Chopped fresh parsley
ingredients.notes: use fresh parsley for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Chopped fresh mint
ingredients.notes: use fresh mint for a unique twist

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: use high-quality salt for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Black pepper
ingredients.notes: use freshly ground black pepper for maximum flavor

instructions: Heat the oil in a large clay pot over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the matcha powder and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the rice and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the chickpeas, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add 2 cups of water to the pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the rice is cooked and creamy.
Stir in the chopped parsley and mint.
Serve the dish hot, garnished with additional parsley and mint if desired.
